with_skill.label: New Skill v2.1
with_skill.description: Neo Team skill with /simplify integration, structured remediation loop (max 2 cycles), and explicit context passing between agents

old_skill.label: Old Skill v1.0
old_skill.description: Original Neo Team skill without /simplify step, no defined remediation loop, and no max cycle limit

evals.description: Add endpoint for create consent record in consent service with citizen_id, purpose, channel, DB save, and audit log
Consent service returns 500 error on GET consent by citizen_id with more than 100 records due to query performance issue
Review code in internal/domain/consent/ before merge to main covering convention, security, and test coverage

analyst_observations.observation: The /simplify step is consistently present in all 3 with_skill runs and absent in all 3 old_skill runs. This is the most reliable differentiator between the two configurations. In with_skill, /simplify is invoked both after Developer implementation AND after remediation fixes, providing two quality gates.
analyst_observations.evidence: with_skill: 3/3 pass simplify-step-present. old_skill: 0/3 pass. In new-feature with_skill, /simplify applied 5 quality fixes. In bugfix with_skill, /simplify extracted reusable parsePagination helper. In code-review with_skill, /simplify removed duplicated validation blocks.

analyst_observations.observation: with_skill implements a structured, bounded remediation loop in all 3 test cases. old_skill shows inconsistent remediation behavior: new-feature and bugfix improvise ad-hoc rework cycles (and explicitly flag missing loop as a skill gap), while code-review completely lacks remediation and just reports findings. The code-review old_skill run is the weakest -- it ends with 'BLOCKED' status and tells the user to fix manually and re-run.
analyst_observations.evidence: with_skill: 3/3 pass remediation-loop-shown. old_skill: 2/3 pass (new-feature and bugfix improvise, code-review fails). All 3 old_skill runs explicitly identify missing remediation loop as a skill gap in their observations tables.

analyst_observations.observation: Both configurations demonstrate good context passing between agents for workflow-specific assertions (correct-workflow-selected, system-analyzer-first, context-passed-between-agents, parallel-review-step, code-reviewer-first). The core orchestration logic for selecting workflows and chaining agent outputs works well in both versions. The difference is in the quality infrastructure (/simplify) and robustness infrastructure (bounded remediation).
analyst_observations.evidence: Workflow-specific assertions pass at same rate: with_skill 9/9, old_skill 9/9. All failures in old_skill are concentrated in the three common assertions (simplify-step-present, remediation-loop-shown, remediation-max-2-cycles).

analyst_observations.observation: with_skill uses ~27% more tokens and ~30% more time but achieves 100% pass rate vs 61.1%. The code-review test case shows the highest cost delta (+47.5% tokens, +58% duration) but also the highest quality improvement (+50pp pass rate). This is because with_skill runs a full remediation cycle with /simplify + re-review for 3 blocking agents, while old_skill just reports findings and stops.
analyst_observations.evidence: Aggregate: with_skill 100734 tokens / 615s, old_skill 79128 tokens / 472s. Per-assertion cost: with_skill = 5596 tokens/pass, old_skill = 7194 tokens/pass (old_skill is actually less efficient per passing assertion).
